data.title: Japan Maintains Economic Recovery Outlook Amid U.S. Trade Policy Concerns
data.description: According to Odaily, the Japanese government remains firm in its assessment that the country's economy is experiencing a moderate recovery. However, it highlights U.S. trade policies as a significant factor to watch, particularly due to U.S. President Donald Trump's proposed tariffs, which introduce uncertainty to the economic outlook. In its February economic report, Japan's Cabinet Office has maintained its overall economic evaluation for the seventh consecutive month. This decision is influenced by ongoing inflation, especially in daily necessities, which continues to dampen consumer spending. The Cabinet Office also emphasized the importance of closely monitoring U.S. policies, particularly in trade, as President Trump is expected to announce more detailed tariff plans. Additionally, the Cabinet Office has revised its view on imports from showing a recent upward trend to being nearly flat.

data.title: Analysts: Safe-haven demand intensifies, gold bullish pattern emerges
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Due to the uncertainty of US President Trump's tariff plan, safe-haven funds continue to flow into gold. Spot gold rose 0.3% on Wednesday, setting a new record high. Jim Wyckoff, senior market analyst at Kitco Metals, said: "Due to the chaotic nature of the Trump administration, safe-haven demand has increased, and we have also seen bullish chart patterns." Analysts at Commerzbank said in a report: "Central bank gold purchases should also continue to support gold prices." (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 96,000 USDT with a 0.29% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 19, 2025, 10:00 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 96,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 96,043.65625 USDT, with a narrowed 0.29% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: UK Inflation Rises, Impacting Central Bank's Rate Decisions
data.description: According to Odaily, Raymond James investment strategist Bart Stone-Carl has indicated that despite the Bank of England's potential view of rising inflation as a temporary issue, January's data shows inflation climbing to 3.0%. This suggests that further reductions in the benchmark interest rate may be delayed. The key challenge for the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee is determining the duration of these price pressures. Two weeks ago, the committee unanimously voted in favor of a rate cut. However, since then, its chief economist, Huw Pill, has cautioned against hasty rate cuts despite weak economic activity. "Today's data confirms that the Bank of England's commitment to gradually easing policy is the right approach," he stated.

data.title: Dollar steady as markets doubt Trump's latest tariff threat will pan out
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Lee Hardman, an analyst at Mitsubishi UFJ Bank, said in a report that Trump's latest tariff threat has little impact on the dollar because the market is optimistic that his plan will not be realized. Trump said on Tuesday that he was considering imposing tariffs of about 25% on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals. He warned that tariffs could "rise significantly within a year." Hardman believes that despite the potentially devastating impact of these plans, the dollar failed to appreciate significantly overnight. This may reflect doubts about whether Trump will put his threats into practice, given that he has not taken concrete actions at the beginning of his second term. (Jinshi)

data.title: Japan still sees modest economic recovery, cautious about U.S. tariffs
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Japanese government is sticking to its view that its economy is recovering moderately, but pointed out that U.S. trade policy is a key factor to watch as Trump's tariff proposals have cast uncertainty over the economic outlook. In its monthly economic report for February, Japan's Cabinet Office maintained its assessment of the overall economy for the seventh consecutive month as persistent inflation, especially for daily necessities, weakened consumption. The Cabinet Office also mentioned the need to keep a close eye on U.S. policies, especially on trade, as U.S. President Trump is expected to make a series of more detailed tariff statements. The Cabinet Office downgraded its view on imports from "showing a recent trend of recovery" to "almost flat." (Jinshi)

data.title: Think XRP Is ‘Crashing’? Analyst Says You Deserve A Slap!
data.description:
XRP has recently witnessed a notable decline, slipping by 5% over the past week. The altcoin dropped below key levels of $2.40 and $2.45, raising concerns among investors. However, a market analyst believes this price shift is not a sign of a crash but rather part of a bullish ascending triangle formation. Related Reading: Cardano Soars Nearly 130% To $30 Billion, Climbs To 9th In Market Cap...
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/think-xrp-is-crashing-analyst-says-you-deserve-a-slap/

data.title: Spot gold hits new highs as trade risks continue to increase gold's appeal
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Driven by safe-haven demand, gold futures rose to near-record highs (the all-time high of $2,968.50 set on February 11). Spot gold has just hit a record high of $2,946.50. ANZ Research analysts said in a report that gold prices rose as the market focus returned to the uncertain economic backdrop. The risk of a full-scale trade war is high, which has stimulated investor demand for safe-haven assets such as gold. (Jinshi)

data.title: Robinhood Crypto Vice President: The platform is relatively strict about listing, mainly considering security and liquidity
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Johann Kerbrat, Vice President of Robinhood Crypto Business, talked about Robinhood's listing standards in Consensus Hong Kong 2025 today.
Johann Kerbrat said that Robinhood's listing process is relatively strict. The platform currently only supports 22 assets. In our listing process, the focus of our discussion is the security and liquidity of the assets. Can we provide them to customers safely? Can we provide sufficient liquidity? Can we provide suitable quotes?
When talking about issues related to meme tokens, Johann Kerbrat also said that many people are discussing meme tokens, but they ignore that other assets are also being used in large quantities. For example, we see many people buying Ethereum or Solana and seeing the strong adoption of stablecoins in institutions. In my opinion, although it is always exciting to talk about the latest meme tokens, we cannot ignore the real adoption of the technology behind them.
